libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property is indicated. For OSGi-related tests, do not port the 1.9 Activator behavior unchanged: in 1.14 Activator.start delegates to OsgiRegistry.getInstance(), which only initializes when Jersey is loaded by a ClassLoader implementing BundleReference; outside OSGi, a no-op assertion may be the meaningful behavior unless the test deliberately simulates that classloader.

deterministic_setup.reason: com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14 declares org.osgi.core 4.2.0 as a provided dependency, and com.sun.jersey.core.osgi.OsgiRegistry imports BundleReference and FrameworkUtil from that API. The existing org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 artifact does not contain BundleReference.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8875 [Automation] java run fails for com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If the old org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 dependency remains alongside org.osgi.core, duplicate org.osgi.framework classes may make classpath behavior order-sensitive; local Gradle verification is authoritative.
Adding the OSGi API fixes the missing BundleReference class but does not by itself make a non-OSGi test environment behave like a real OSGi runtime.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jersey core 1.14 has an OSGi code path that imports org.osgi.framework.BundleReference from its provided org.osgi.core 4.2.0 dependency; the older osgi_R4_core:1.0 test dependency lacks that class and caused the observed java-run failure.
